RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 116-3, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Brandon Phelps, a Republican, represents Johnson County (District 54) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
A longtime resident of Johnson County, Phelps is a graduate of Warrensburg High School and a successful entrepreneur. He has over 30 years of experience in the auto sales industry where he has honed his skills in customer service and business management. For the past 17 years, he has owned and operated Sonic Lawn and Landscape, providing quality services to his community.
Phelps has been an active leader in the Republican Party, serving as chairman of the Johnson County Republican Central Committee since 2018 and the 17th Judicial District from 2020 to 2024. His experience in these roles has deepened his commitment to representing the values and priorities of his constituents.
Beyond his legislative duties, Phelps is dedicated to his family. He is happily married to his wife, Angela, and together they share two children, Morgan and Allie. He takes pride in being a devoted husband and father, balancing his professional and personal responsibilities with a strong sense of purpose.
Phelps is passionate about fostering economic growth, supporting small businesses, and ensuring a brighter future for the families of Johnson County. In his free time, he enjoys outdoor activities, spending time with his family, and engaging in community events that strengthen local ties.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to gender designation on driver's licenses, nondriver's licenses, and permits
Creates provisions relating to air ambulance memberships
Requires law enforcement agencies to report the immigration status of criminal offenders to the department of public safety
Requires health care facilities to post a sign warning of the potential criminal consequences of assaulting health care professionals
Modifies provisions relating to sex designation on driver's licenses, nondriver's licenses, and permits
Requires school districts and charter schools to notify parents if schools use tutoring services or programs owned by a foreign entity
